SEARCH
Package server.dao

Class MemberDAO

java.lang.Object
server.dao.MemberDAO
public class MemberDAO extends Object
Data access object for member registration: the visitor base row plus the subscriber and guide extension rows that a SERVICE_REP creates.

Follows the same conventions as AuthDAO and ReservationDAO: each method opens a short-lived Connection from DBConnection, runs parameterized statements, and signals failure through the return value rather than propagating SQL exceptions. A subscriber or guide is a visitor with one extra row, so callers first upsertVisitor(long, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, boolean) the base identity and then add the role row.

Duplicate detection (already a subscriber / already a guide) relies on the extension tables' visitor_id primary key: the role-row insert is attempted and a SQLIntegrityConstraintViolationException is caught and reported as false. This mirrors AuthDAO.lock(long, java.lang.String) and avoids the check-then-insert race a separate existence query would open.

  • Constructor Details

    • MemberDAO

      public MemberDAO()

  • Method Details

    • visitorExists

      public boolean visitorExists(long id)
      Checks whether a visitor row already exists for the given national ID.
      Parameters:
      id - the visitor (national) id to probe
      Returns:
      true if a matching visitor row exists, false if none matches or the query fails

    • upsertVisitor

      public void upsertVisitor(long id, String fullName, String phone, String email, boolean isSubscriber)
      Find-or-create the base visitor row for a national ID.

      visitor.id is an assigned national ID (not auto-increment), so a new visitor is inserted with the id supplied from the form; re-registering an existing visitor updates their contact details instead of failing on the primary key. The is_subscriber flag is only ever raised, never lowered: when registering a subscriber (isSubscriber == true) it is set TRUE, but registering an existing subscriber as a guide (isSubscriber == false) leaves their subscription flag untouched so the member discount keeps firing.

      Parameters:
      id - the visitor's assigned national id (primary key)
      fullName - the visitor's display name
      phone - the visitor's phone number
      email - the visitor's email address
      isSubscriber - whether to mark the visitor as a subscriber; false never clears an existing subscription

    • upsertContact

      public void upsertContact(long id, String email, String phone)
      Find-or-create a visitor by national ID, saving only their contact details.

      Used by the booking path so a reservation's visitor_id foreign key always resolves and the visitor's email/phone are on file for notifications. Unlike upsertVisitor(long, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, boolean), this never writes full_name or is_subscriber: a brand-new visitor is inserted with those left at their defaults (full_name NULL, is_subscriber FALSE), while an existing visitor has only their two contact columns updated. That matters when a registered subscriber re-books — their name and subscription flag (and thus the member discount) must survive the upsert untouched.

      Parameters:
      id - the visitor's assigned national id (primary key)
      email - the visitor's email address
      phone - the visitor's phone number

    • subscriberFamilySize

      public int subscriberFamilySize(long visitorId)
      Returns the registered family size for a subscriber, or zero when the visitor is not a subscriber.
      Parameters:
      visitorId - the visitor's national id
      Returns:
      family size for subscribers, otherwise 0

    • registerSubscriber

      public boolean registerSubscriber(long visitorId, int familySize)
      Promotes a visitor to a subscriber: marks visitor.is_subscriber TRUE and inserts the subscriber row with today's join date.

      The caller must have created the base visitor (see upsertVisitor(long, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, boolean)) first. The subscriber insert is attempted directly; a duplicate primary key means the visitor is already a subscriber, which is caught and reported as false rather than raising an error.

      Parameters:
      visitorId - the visitor's national id
      familySize - the subscriber's family size
      Returns:
      true if a new subscriber row was created, false if the visitor was already a subscriber or the insert failed

    • registerGuide

      public boolean registerGuide(long visitorId, int registeredByUserId)
      Registers a visitor as a group guide by inserting the guide row with today's approval date and the service rep who registered them.

      The caller must have created the base visitor (see upsertVisitor(long, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, boolean)) first. The insert is attempted directly; a duplicate primary key means the visitor is already a guide, which is caught and reported as false.

      Parameters:
      visitorId - the visitor's national id
      registeredByUserId - the id of the SERVICE_REP performing the registration
      Returns:
      true if a new guide row was created, false if the visitor was already a guide or the insert failed
